notes: This file records source-reviewed reconciliation findings. Records absent from this file remain unverified and must not be treated as rules-authoritative.
Force Lightning retains its tiered damage progression; the confirmed drift is its damage type, which is Force damage.
The pack remains unchanged in Phase 2. Later phases will migrate source-reviewed records into system.resolution.

battle-strike.name: Battle Strike
battle-strike.currentPackFinding: Verify that the compendium record is named Battle Strike and preserves the tiered extra-damage dice.
battle-strike.runtimeFinding: Current ForcePowerEffectsEngine contains a name-based Force Strike handler that models a flat damage bonus and may not match Battle Strike.
requiredCorrections: Represent the +1 Force bonus to the next attack roll.
Represent tiered extra damage dice on that same attack if it hits.
Expire after the next qualifying attack.
Remove reliance on a mismatched Force Strike name handler.

farseeing.name: Farseeing
farseeing.currentPackFinding: Current text allows observing a known place or person like a remote camera. The printed power targets a known creature and provides limited information based on the check.
farseeing.runtimeFinding: No dedicated source-aligned information resolver exists.
requiredCorrections: Target a creature rather than a place.
Compare Use the Force against Will Defense.
Represent failure, basic information, the 24-hour repeat restriction, and the Force Point improvement.

force-disarm.name: Force Disarm
force-disarm.currentPackFinding: Current record describes an opposed Strength or Will contest. The printed rule substitutes Use the Force for the normal disarm attack roll.
requiredCorrections: Use the Use the Force check in place of the normal disarm attack roll.
Preserve the rest of the disarm rules.
Represent the Force Point option that damages or destroys the weapon.

force-grip.name: Force Grip
force-grip.currentPackFinding: Damage alone is insufficient; the power also restricts the target's next-turn actions and can be maintained.
force-grip.runtimeFinding: Current name-based immobilize handling returns no effect and direct Force damage is unwired.
requiredCorrections: Compare against Fortitude Defense.
Preserve tiered damage.
Apply the successful-use action restriction.
Support concentration and a new check each maintained round.
Represent the Force Point damage increase.

force-lightning.name: Force Lightning
force-lightning.currentPackFinding: The tiered damage progression is correct. The confirmed drift is that the damage must be typed as Force damage rather than electricity/lightning damage.
force-lightning.runtimeFinding: Direct Force-power damage is not yet routed through the canonical damage pipeline.
requiredCorrections: Keep the existing tiered damage progression.
Set damageType to force.
Keep condition or Force Point riders as separate structured outcomes.
Do not infer electricity damage from the power name.

force-slam.name: Force Slam
force-slam.currentPackFinding: Current damage profile infers kinetic damage and leaves area geometry unresolved. The printed power deals Force damage in a 6-square cone.
force-slam.runtimeFinding: No schema-driven area Force-damage resolver exists.
requiredCorrections: Use a 6-square cone.
Compare one check against each target's Fortitude Defense.
Deal 4d6 Force damage on a hit and half on a miss.
Apply prone only on a hit.
Represent the Force Point +2d6 option.

force-stun.name: Force Stun
force-stun.currentPackFinding: A generic stunned condition does not represent the printed margin-based condition-track movement.
requiredCorrections: Compare against Will Defense.
Move the target down the condition track based on success margin.
Do not model the result as a fixed-duration stunned condition.

force-thrust.name: Force Thrust
force-thrust.currentPackFinding: The power is primarily forced movement; HP damage occurs conditionally when the target collides with a larger object.
force-thrust.runtimeFinding: Current immobilize handling does not implement forced movement or collision damage.
requiredCorrections: Resolve the opposed Strength interaction.
Apply forced movement.
Create damage only when the collision condition is met.
Represent the Force Point option separately.

move-object.name: Move Object
move-object.currentPackFinding: Current record retains size tiers but omits the complete hurling damage and secondary-target resolution structure.
move-object.runtimeFinding: Current Move Object effect handler intentionally returns no effect and has no mode selection.
requiredCorrections: Represent move, hold, hurl-object, and hurl-creature modes independently.
Preserve size-based tiers and size-based damage.
Support unwilling-target resistance and secondary target defense.
Support damage to both hurling participants where applicable.
Support concentration, Force Point, Destiny Point, and flight/hover interactions.

rebuke.name: Rebuke
rebuke.currentPackFinding: Current compendium record describes an offensive daze power. The printed power is a reaction used to negate or redirect another Force power.
rebuke.runtimeFinding: No reaction-opposed negation and redirection resolver exists.
requiredCorrections: Trigger when the user is targeted by a Force power.
Compare against the originating Force power check.
Negate on a matching or higher result.
Redirect when the result exceeds the originating check by the required margin.
Represent chaining and Force Point interactions.

surge.name: Surge
surge.currentPackFinding: Current record grants an additional move action. The printed power grants tiered Jump and speed bonuses.
surge.runtimeFinding: Current ForcePowerEffectsEngine incorrectly routes Surge to a damage bonus.
requiredCorrections: Remove the extra move action description.
Represent tiered Jump bonuses.
Represent tiered speed increases.
Represent the Force Point improvement.
Remove the damage-bonus runtime behavior.

vital-transfer.name: Vital Transfer
vital-transfer.currentPackFinding: Current record uses healing dice and invented condition-removal tiers. The printed power scales healing from the target's level and inflicts a cost on the user.
vital-transfer.runtimeFinding: Healing powers currently create no direct healing transaction.
requiredCorrections: Replace healing dice with target-level multipliers.
Apply the user's self-damage cost separately from healing.
Represent the Force Point option that prevents the self-damage cost.
Represent the Destiny Point condition-track improvement.
Remove unsupported condition-removal tiers.
